alternatives.name: Apache Airflow UI
alternatives.summary: The browser UI is the primary place for operators to inspect DAGs, DAG runs, task instances, pools, and scheduler health.

alternatives.name: Airflow REST API
alternatives.summary: The stable REST API exposes the operational data directly, but callers still need to manage auth, pagination, envelopes, filtering, and repeatable command output.

alternatives.name: Airflow CLI
alternatives.summary: The native Airflow CLI is useful on Airflow hosts, but data engineers and agents often need a remote read-only operations surface from their own terminal.

gaps: Data engineers need fast readouts for failed DAG runs and task instances without opening the Airflow UI for every incident.
The Airflow API returns resource-named list envelopes such as dags, dag_runs, task_instances, and pools; generic API clients usually do not turn those into searchable local records.
Agents need compact, non-interactive commands that can answer pipeline health questions without mutating Airflow state.
Local validation should prove the normal triage path: authenticate, check health, list DAG runs, inspect task instances, sync metadata, and search locally.
patterns: Start with Airflow health and version before investigating pipeline failures.
Use DAG run filters by state and date window to narrow incident scope.
Inspect task instances after locating a failed DAG run so the failing task is explicit.
Sync metadata locally when an engineer or agent needs repeated search without repeatedly paging the Airflow API.
Keep credentials in environment variables, local config, or MCP secret prompts; never commit tokens or Airflow passwords.

novel_features.name: Airflow health readout
novel_features.description: Checks Airflow health so an engineer can confirm the webserver, scheduler, triggerer, DAG processor, and metadatabase before drilling into pipeline failures.
novel_features.rationale: Data pipeline incidents often start with the question of whether Airflow itself is healthy or whether a specific DAG failed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: This gives agents and engineers a small first command for operational triage.

novel_features.name: DAG inventory
novel_features.description: Lists DAG metadata such as active and paused state so teams can see what Airflow is managing.
novel_features.rationale: A pipeline may be missing, paused, stale, or inactive before any deeper task-level debugging is needed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: This keeps DAG discovery scriptable and readable outside the Airflow UI.

novel_features.name: DAG run triage
novel_features.description: Filters DAG run history by state and time window to identify failed, queued, or delayed pipeline runs.
novel_features.rationale: Data engineers usually debug pipelines by finding the failed or late run first, then inspecting tasks.
novel_features.example: airflow-admin-pp-cli dags dag-runs list daily_warehouse_refresh --state failed --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: This matches the common daily-refresh and overnight-load triage path.

novel_features.name: Task instance inspection
novel_features.description: Shows task-level state, try count, and timing inside a DAG run.
novel_features.rationale: A failed DAG run is only the starting point; the useful answer is usually the exact failing task and retry state.
novel_features.example: airflow-admin-pp-cli dags dag-runs list-task-instances daily_warehouse_refresh scheduled__2026-06-14T00:00:00+00:00 --state failed --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: This lets agents report the failing step instead of only saying that a pipeline failed.

novel_features.name: Local Airflow metadata search
novel_features.description: Syncs Airflow metadata into a local SQLite store for offline search and lightweight analysis.
novel_features.rationale: Airflow metadata is useful for repeated incident analysis, but constantly paging the API is slow and noisy.
novel_features.example: airflow-admin-pp-cli sync --resources dags,pools --agent && airflow-admin-pp-cli search failed --data-source local --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: This turns Airflow operations data into a small local evidence set for engineers and agents.

novel_features_built.name: Airflow health readout
novel_features_built.description: Checks Airflow health so an engineer can confirm the webserver, scheduler, triggerer, DAG processor, and metadatabase before drilling into pipeline failures.
novel_features_built.rationale: Validated against Apache Airflow 3.0.0 in a disposable local Docker container.
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: This gives agents and engineers a small first command for operational triage.

novel_features_built.name: DAG inventory
novel_features_built.description: Lists DAG metadata such as active and paused state so teams can see what Airflow is managing.
novel_features_built.rationale: Validated with live DAG metadata from the local Airflow container.
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: This keeps DAG discovery scriptable and readable outside the Airflow UI.

novel_features_built.name: DAG run triage
novel_features_built.description: Filters DAG run history by state and time window to identify failed, queued, or delayed pipeline runs.
novel_features_built.rationale: Validated with a disposable test DAG containing an intentionally failed run.
novel_features_built.example: airflow-admin-pp-cli dags dag-runs list pp_validation_daily_refresh --state failed --agent
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: This matches the common daily-refresh and overnight-load triage path.

novel_features_built.name: Task instance inspection
novel_features_built.description: Shows task-level state, try count, and timing inside a DAG run.
novel_features_built.rationale: Validated by listing successful and failed task instances in the disposable validation DAG run.
novel_features_built.example: airflow-admin-pp-cli dags dag-runs list-task-instances pp_validation_daily_refresh <dag_run_id> --state failed --agent
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: This lets agents report the failing step instead of only saying that a pipeline failed.

novel_features_built.name: Local Airflow metadata search
novel_features_built.description: Syncs Airflow metadata into a local SQLite store for offline search and lightweight analysis.
novel_features_built.rationale: Validated after adding explicit support for Airflow list envelopes and Airflow identifier fields.
novel_features_built.example: airflow-admin-pp-cli sync --resources dags,pools --agent && airflow-admin-pp-cli search validation --data-source local --agent
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: This turns Airflow operations data into a small local evidence set for engineers and agents.

narrative.headline: A read-first Airflow operations CLI for data pipeline triage.
narrative.value_prop: Airflow schedules and monitors data pipelines, but engineers still need a fast command-line way to inspect DAG health, failed runs, task instances, pools, and searchable metadata from outside the browser UI.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set AIRFLOW_ADMIN_BASE_URL when Airflow is not running at http://localhost:8080. Store a JWT access token with auth set-token or set AIRFLOW_ADMIN_BEARER_AUTH in the shell or MCP client's secret prompt.

quickstart.comment: Get a JWT token from a local Airflow environment that allows username/password auth.
Check whether Airflow services are healthy.

troubleshoots.fix: Run auth set-token with a valid Airflow JWT or set AIRFLOW_ADMIN_BEARER_AUTH in the shell.
Set AIRFLOW_ADMIN_BASE_URL to the Airflow webserver/API URL and run doctor.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for read-only Airflow inspection, pipeline incident triage, pool checks, metadata sync, and local search. Do not use it to deploy DAGs or mutate Airflow state.

recipes.title: Check the scheduler before debugging a DAG
recipes.explanation: Returns Airflow component health in compact JSON.

recipes.title: Find the failed task in a DAG run
recipes.explanation: Shows the failed task instance, try count, and timing.
